description: Diagnostic-only timing canary manifest for private act passive-drain and passive effect execution/error-routing gates; public comparable timing remains blocked until public act, test-renderer, effect, and error-surface gates are green.

acceptedGate.notes: Package-private React act metadata admits renderer-backed act drain diagnostics that consume accepted Scheduler, reconciler, and test-renderer records while public React.act, scheduler timing, queued work execution, effect execution, and renderer root compatibility remain blocked.

The private react-test-renderer act scheduler gate accepts passive flush metadata, private sync-flush continuation metadata, private passive callback metadata, and private root-output diagnostics without executing public act work or claiming public test-renderer compatibility.

Private reconciler canaries execute passive destroy callbacks before mount-create callbacks under explicit test controls and record deterministic callback metadata while public effect execution, scheduler-driven passive execution, and public act compatibility remain blocked.

Private passive effect error-routing canaries preserve destroy-before-create error order and schedule root error capture metadata without invoking root error callbacks or enabling public act error aggregation.

Public React.act remains blocked behind renderer roots, passive effects, act continuation flushing, and compatibility claims, so private act passive-drain timing cannot become public comparable timing evidence.

Public react-test-renderer act compatibility remains blocked because public act queue draining, public scheduler flush execution, passive callback execution, root request execution, and host-output commit compatibility are not admitted.

Public test-renderer error-surface compatibility remains blocked; passive effect error routing is private root-error metadata only and does not prove public callback invocation, console, or act aggregation behavior.

milestones.description: Private act passive-drain and passive effect execution/error-routing timing canaries may collect diagnostic timing for admitted private gates only.
milestones.blockedReason: These timing canaries are admitted only for private act passive-drain, passive callback execution, and passive error-routing diagnostics; public act behavior, scheduler-driven passive execution, effect compatibility, error-surface compatibility, and comparable timing remain blocked.

milestones.description: Public comparable timing promotion remains blocked until public React.act, react-test-renderer act, passive effect, and error-surface gates become green.
milestones.blockedReason: Comparable public timing remains blocked until public React.act, react-test-renderer act, passive effect execution, passive error routing, scheduler flushing, renderer roots, and error-surface gates are green-admitted and claim compatibility.

scenarios.area: React private renderer-backed act passive-drain timing canary
scenarios.blockedReason: The act passive-drain timing canary covers only private renderer-backed act drain diagnostics, accepted internal queues, passive flush metadata, and test-renderer root-output metadata; public React.act, public react-test-renderer act, queued work execution, passive effect execution, scheduler timing, and comparable timing remain blocked.

scenarios.area: Reconciler private passive effect mount/unmount execution timing canary
scenarios.blockedReason: The passive effect mount/unmount execution timing canary covers only private test-control destroy and mount-create callback executors with deterministic unmount-before-mount ordering; public effect execution, scheduler-driven passive execution, public act compatibility, hook facade compatibility, and comparable timing remain blocked.

scenarios.area: Reconciler private passive effect error-routing timing canary
scenarios.blockedReason: The passive effect error-routing timing canary covers only private destroy and mount-create error metadata, root error capture scheduling, and blocked root callback/act aggregation records; public error-surface compatibility, public act error aggregation, callback invocation claims, and comparable timing remain blocked.
